activation_mode.mechanism: activation_modes extension: always (unconditional), conditional (glob patterns), model (AI decides), manual (explicit toggle)

auto_memory.mechanism: auto_generated_memories: Windsurf AI generates persistent memories stored in .windsurf/memories/ as rule-like files

cross_provider_recognition.mechanism: agents_md_auto_scoping: Windsurf reads AGENTS.md files from other providers and applies them with workspace-aware scoping

file_imports.mechanism: No @-import or file inclusion syntax documented for Windsurf rule files

hierarchical_loading.mechanism: Windsurf rule files are global or workspace-scoped; no subdirectory precedence model documented

providerExtensions.name: Four activation modes via trigger frontmatter
providerExtensions.description: Workspace rules declare an activation mode in frontmatter via the `trigger` field. Four modes exist: `always_on` (included in every system prompt), `model_decision` (description always shown; full content loaded on demand when the model judges it relevant), `glob` (applied when Cascade reads or edits files matching the `globs` pattern), and `manual` (excluded from system prompt until @-mentioned by name in the Cascade input box). The global rules file and root-level AGENTS.md do not use frontmatter — they are always on.

providerExtensions.name: AGENTS.md: location-based auto-scoping without frontmatter
providerExtensions.description: AGENTS.md (or agents.md) files use plain markdown with no frontmatter. Windsurf infers the activation mode from file location: a root-level AGENTS.md is treated as always-on; any subdirectory AGENTS.md is treated as a glob rule scoped to that directory's subtree (auto-generated pattern `<directory>/**`). This provides simple directory-specific guidance without any frontmatter configuration.

providerExtensions.name: Global, workspace, and system rule scopes
providerExtensions.description: Rules exist at three user-accessible scopes plus an enterprise system scope. Global: single file at ~/.codeium/windsurf/memories/global_rules.md, always-on, 6,000 character limit. Workspace: one .md file per rule in .windsurf/rules/, each with its own activation mode, 12,000 character limit per file. System (Enterprise): OS-specific read-only directories deployed by IT — macOS /Library/Application Support/Windsurf/rules/*.md, Linux/WSL /etc/windsurf/rules/*.md, Windows C:\ProgramData\Windsurf\rules\*.md. Workspace rules are also discovered in subdirectories and parent directories up to the git root, with deduplication across multiple open folders.

providerExtensions.name: Auto-generated memories distinct from rules
providerExtensions.description: Memories are a separate mechanism from Rules. Cascade auto-generates memories during conversations when it encounters context worth remembering, and stores them at ~/.codeium/windsurf/memories/ (workspace-scoped, not committed to git). Users can also ask Cascade to create a memory explicitly. Memories do not consume credits. They are distinct from Rules, which are user-authored and version-controlled. The docs recommend preferring Rules or AGENTS.md over memories for durable, team-shareable knowledge.
